title: install --update clobbers user schema customizations; no customization-preserving merge
description: install --update overwrites each installed schema with the catalog copy — verbatim on a same-version resync, migrate-or-refuse on a version gap — so a user's edits to a shipped block schema are lost on update. Block item data is preserved (S1) and a user-added custom block kind is untouched (no catalog counterpart), but edits to a catalog-shipped schema have no merge path: update is replace.

evidence.reference: resyncSchema overwrites the installed schema with the catalog version on install --update; same-version resync is a verbatim overwrite, version-gap is migrate-or-refuse — neither reconciles local schema edits

impact: A non-developer user who tailors a shipped schema must choose: take the update and lose the customization, or run plain install (never-clobber) and forgo the update. Tailoring a catalog schema is fragile against the next --update.
proposed_resolution: On install --update, three-way merge the catalog schema change against the user's local schema edits (base = the previously-installed catalog version), preserving non-conflicting local edits and surfacing conflicts rather than overwriting; or an equivalent overlay that re-applies recorded user customizations after the catalog refresh.
